expectations.text: Uses the emitter pattern: a node has a back-edge (downstream step routes back to the emitter), creating a cycle in the Mermaid graph
Cyclic entry node is marked with stadium shape (e.g., `emit([...])`) because auto-detection fails on cycles
Emitter step maintains a cursor in `LOCAL` across re-entries (reads `$LOCAL` / writes `LOCAL: {...}`)
Emitter publishes the current PR item to `GLOBAL` so downstream steps read it generically (e.g., `{{ GLOBAL.item.* }}`)
An agent step (plain prose) performs the review summary and references `GLOBAL.*` via Liquid
expectations.evidence: ## review (lines 60-74) is plain prose and uses `{{ GLOBAL.item.* }}`

expectations.text: Has a `done` / terminal branch out of the emitter so the loop can exit (e.g., `emit -->|done| ...`)
expectations.evidence: Line 23: `emit -->|done| summary`; emit emits `RESULT: $(jo edge=done)` when cursor exhausted

expectations.text: Every node in the mermaid flow has a matching `## <id>` heading and vice versa
expectations.evidence: emit, review, print, summary all present in both flow and steps
